rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in the appropriate AADL scope: component type for interface assumptions/guarantees, implementation for bindings.
Respect AADL scope boundaries: component types for port-based contracts, implementations for subcomponent-level bindings.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL architecture integrity during AGREE fusion by aligning annex content with component type and implementation scopes.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the analyzed AADL model.
Ensure AGREE annex content reflects the authoritative AADL structure for ports, subcomponents, and connections.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use valid AADL scope for AGREE declarations: component types for interface-level assumptions/guarantees, implementations for bindings.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level AGREE bindings in component type scopes or vice versa.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or AADL properties as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed in the model.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE syntax and annex structure (covered by KSYN-1)
AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns (covered by KDEF_CARDS)
AGREE contract semantics and requirement mapping (covered by requirement_analyst)
